title: Motion Sickness Solutions: Top 10 Herbal Creams

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerol is good for motion sickness because it helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea by slowing down the movement of the digestive system, which can be disturbed during travel.
Shogaol is good for motion sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm the stomach and reduce nausea.

Zingiberene is good for motion sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to calm the digestive system and reduce the symptoms of nausea and dizziness caused by motion sickness.
Shogaols are good for motion sickness because they have been shown to help reduce nausea and vomiting by blocking the action of a chemical in the brain that makes people feel queasy.
Gingerols are good for motion sickness because they help to reduce nausea and vomiting by calming the stomach and reducing inflammation in the digestive system.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale, also known as ginger, has been used for centuries to help with motion sickness. The active compounds in ginger, called gingerols and shogaols, work to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. When you're on a boat or car, your body gets mixed signals from your senses, which can cause your stomach to feel queasy. Ginger helps to settle your stomach and ease the discomfort of motion sickness. It does this by slowing down the movement of food through the digestive system and by blocking the release of chemicals in the stomach that can make you feel sick. This makes ginger a popular natural remedy for motion sickness, and it's often found in creams and other products that can be applied to the skin to help alleviate symptoms.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Citrus reticulata herbal creams that help with motion sickness, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Citrus reticulata peel, coconut oil, shea butter, beeswax, vitamin E oil, and lavender essential oil. Begin by infusing the dried Citrus reticulata peel in a carrier oil, such as coconut oil, by placing the peel in a clean glass jar and covering it with the oil, then letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar occasionally. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the peel. Next, in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water, melt the coconut oil, shea butter, and beeswax, stirring occasionally until smooth. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly, then stir in the infused Citrus reticulata oil, vitamin E oil, and lavender essential oil. Pour the mixture into a clean container and let it cool completely before use. This herbal cream can be applied to the wrists, temples, or behind the ears before traveling to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for motion sickness

Kaempferol is good for motion sickness because it helps to reduce nausea and vomiting by acting as a potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent that protects the stomach and intestines from damage caused by stress and inflammation.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba cream can help with motion sickness because it improves blood flow to the brain. When you're on a boat or in a car, your body gets mixed signals and it's hard for your brain to understand what's going on. Ginkgo biloba helps your brain get the right information by making sure blood vessels open up and work properly. This can reduce dizziness and nausea. Ginkgo biloba also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which can help you feel more relaxed when you're feeling queasy. Additionally, some people think that ginkgo biloba can help your body adjust to changes in your environment, like when you're on a boat in rough waters. The cream is applied to the skin, so it absorbs quickly and starts working fast.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Citrus reticulata herbal creams that help with motion sickness, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You'll need dried Citrus reticulata leaves, which can be purchased online or at local health food stores, coconut oil, beeswax, shea butter, and a few drops of peppermint essential oil. First, melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler over low heat, then add the shea butter and stir until it's fully incorporated. Remove the mixture from the heat and let it cool slightly before adding the dried Citrus reticulata leaves, which have been steeped in a hot water bath to release their active compounds. Let the mixture infuse for about an hour, or until the leaves have reached your desired potency. After straining the leaves from the mixture, add a few drops of peppermint essential oil to help calm the stomach. Pour the mixture into small containers and let it cool completely before using it as a topical cream to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal creams for motion sickness

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata cream, also known as passionflower cream, can help with motion sickness. The active ingredient, harmine, is a natural compound found in the passionflower plant. It has a calming effect on the nerves and brain, which can help to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness. When you're on a boat or in a car, your body gets conflicting signals, causing your balance and vision to be off. The harmine in the cream helps to calm your nervous system, making it easier to focus and reducing the feeling of nausea. The cream is also applied to the skin, which can be more effective than taking medication orally because it gets to work faster and is less likely to have side effects.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Citrus reticulata herbal creams that help with motion sickness, you'll need to combine the right ingredients and follow a few simple steps. First, you'll need to infuse dried Citrus reticulata leaves in a carrier oil, such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil, by placing the leaves in a clean glass jar and covering them with the oil. Let this mixture sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every few days, until the oil has taken on a light yellow color and a citrusy aroma. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the solids. Next, mix a small amount of the infused oil with a thickening agent, such as beeswax or shea butter, and a base oil, like coconut oil or olive oil, in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Heat the mixture until the beeswax or shea butter has melted, then remove it from the heat and let it cool until it reaches a thick, creamy consistency. Once the cream has cooled and thickened, you can transfer it to a clean glass jar and store it in the refrigerator until you're ready to use it. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to your wrists or temples before traveling, and you may find that it helps to alleviate the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedies.image_alt: Passiflora incarnata herbal creams for motion sickness

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis cream is often used to help with motion sickness because it contains valerenic acid, a chemical that affects the brain and nervous system. When you're on a boat or in a car, your body gets mixed signals from your senses, causing queasiness and nausea. Valerian root cream may help calm your nervous system and reduce this conflict. It works by slowing down your heart rate and lowering your blood pressure, which can help ease the feeling of being sick. Some people also find that valerian root helps them relax and feel less anxious, which can contribute to reduced motion sickness symptoms. This is likely because the cream's active ingredients interact with the brain's natural chemicals, helping to regulate and calm your body's response to motion.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Citrus reticulata herbal creams that help with motion sickness, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you will need to gather dried Citrus reticulata leaves and peel, which have natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness. Next, you will need to make an herbal infusion by steeping the dried leaves and peel in a carrier oil, such as coconut or sweet almond oil, in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water. Let the mixture steep for at least two hours or overnight, allowing the herbal properties to infuse into the oil. Once the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl, discarding the solids. Finally, mix the infused oil with a natural emollient, such as beeswax or shea butter, to create a thick and luxurious cream that can be applied topically to the skin to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Avena sativa cream is a natural remedy that can help with motion sickness. This cream is made from the dried flowers of the oat plant, and it has been used for centuries to calm the stomach and ease nausea. When you're on a boat, plane, or car, and you feel queasy and dizzy, the Avena sativa cream can help. It works by reducing the stress and anxiety that can make motion sickness worse. The cream also helps to regulate your body's natural rhythms and calm your nervous system, which can become overactive when you're feeling queasy. By applying the cream to your skin, you can get relief from the symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness, headaches, and stomach discomfort.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Citrus reticulata herbal creams that help with motion sickness, you'll need a few ingredients and some basic equipment. First, you'll need to dry the peel of the Citrus reticulata fruit, also known as a mandarin or tangerine, by placing it in a low-temperature oven at 150-200°F (65-90°C) for 2-3 hours. Once dry, you can grind the peel into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle. Next, you'll need to infuse the powder into a carrier oil, such as coconut or sweet almond oil, by mixing 1 part powder with 2-3 parts oil in a small saucepan and heating it over low heat for 2-3 hours, or by letting it sit in a cool place for 2-3 days. After the infusion, you can strain the oil and mix it with a base cream, such as shea or cocoa butter, and some essential oils like ginger or peppermint, which can help to ease nausea and calm the stomach. Finally, you can fill small jars with the finished cream and store them in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months.
remedies.image_alt: Avena sativa herbal creams for motion sickness

remedies.remedy_desc: Piper nigrum, also known as black pepper, has been used for years to help with motion sickness. The cream form of piper nigrum contains a chemical called piperine, which is known to affect the brain's nerve cells in a way that helps reduce nausea. When you're feeling queasy on a boat or in a car, piperine works to calm down the brain's signals that make you feel sick. It also helps to reduce inflammation in the stomach, which can be a major contributor to motion sickness. Furthermore, piper nigrum cream has been shown to have a natural effect on the body's balance system, which can be thrown off by motion sickness. By using piper nigrum cream, you may be able to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness and feel more comfortable when traveling.
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make a Citrus reticulata herbal cream that helps with motion sickness, you'll first need to gather the necessary ingredients. Start by drying the peels of Citrus reticulata, also known as a mandarin or tangerine, in a low-temperature oven at 150-200°F (65-90°C) for 2-3 hours or by placing them in a dehydrator on the lowest setting for 6-8 hours. Once dried, grind the peels into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or a spice grinder. Next, combine 2 tablespoons of the dried and powdered peels with 2 tablespoons of a carrier oil, such as coconut or sweet almond oil, in a small saucepan. Heat the mixture over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the mixture reaches a temperature of 160-180°F (71-82°C). Remove the saucepan from the heat and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before straining the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean bowl. Allow the mixture to cool completely before transferring it to a small bowl or tin. Finally, add 1-2 tablespoons of beeswax to the mixture and mix well before spooning the cream into a clean jar or tin. This Citrus reticulata herbal cream can be applied to the skin as needed to help alleviate motion sickness symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Piper nigrum herbal creams for motion sickness

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Citrus reticulata herbal cream that helps with motion sickness, start by infusing the dried peel of Citrus reticulata (also known as a tangor or mandarin orange) in a carrier oil like coconut or sweet almond oil. This infusion is done by placing the dried peel in a clean glass jar, then covering it with the carrier oil, and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. After the infusion is complete, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar, discarding the dried peel. Next, mix the infused oil with a rich moisturizing base like shea butter or beeswax, and a stabilizer like vitamin E oil or a preservative, to create a smooth and stable cream. Finally, transfer the cream to a clean glass jar and store it in the fridge to prolong its shelf life, allowing it to thicken and set over time. remedies.remedy_desc: Piper methysticum creams are used to help people with motion sickness. The active ingredient, kava, is a plant extract that has been used for a long time to calm the body and mind. When you're on a boat or in a car and feel queasy, your body is trying to adjust to the movement. The kava in the cream helps to calm your body and reduce the feeling of queasiness. remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Citrus reticulata herbal creams that help with motion sickness, you will need some basic ingredients and equipment. First, you will need to dry and powder the peel of Citrus reticulata, also known as the mandarin orange, as it has been traditionally used to treat nausea and vomiting. To do this, slice the peel thinly and place it in a low-temperature oven at 150-200 degrees Fahrenheit for 1-2 hours, or until it is dry and crumbly. Next, mix the powdered peel with a carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a ratio of 1 part peel to 10 parts oil. Heat the mixture gently over low heat, stirring occasionally, until the peel is fully incorporated into the oil. Once the mixture has cooled, add a few drops of a natural emulsifier such as beeswax or candelilla wax to thicken the cream, and mix well. Finally, pour the mixture into a glass jar and store it in a cool, dark place for up to 6 months. To use the cream, simply apply a small amount to your skin before traveling or during periods of motion, and massage it in well.

intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a good remedy for motion sickness because they contain natural ingredients that help to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. One of the key herbs found in these creams is Zingiber officinale, or ginger. Ginger has been shown to have anti-inflammatory properties and can help to settle the stomach, reducing the symptoms of motion sickness. It can also help to relax the digestive system and reduce vomiting.  Another herb commonly used in motion sickness creams is Ginkgo biloba. This herb is known to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the blood vessels. This can help to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness by improving blood flow to the brain and reducing nausea.  Fixing motion sickness is important because it can severely impact daily life. If you experience motion sickness, you may avoid traveling by car, boat, or plane, which can limit your social and professional opportunities. You may also feel anxious or stressed about traveling, which can have a negative impact on your mental health. By using herbal creams containing ginger and Ginkgo biloba, you can reduce the symptoms of motion sickness and improve your quality of life. This can help you to feel more confident and relaxed when traveling, and can allow you to participate in activities and experiences that you may have otherwise avoided.
supplementary_best: For motion sickness, the best combination of herbal creams includes Zingiber officinale, Lavandula angustifolia, and Avena sativa. Zingiber officinale, or ginger, helps to calm the digestive system and reduce nausea. It does this by reducing inflammation and blocking the production of a chemical that causes stomach upset. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, has a soothing effect on the mind and body, helping to reduce anxiety and stress that can contribute to motion sickness. It also has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to regulate the body's response to motion. Avena sativa, or oat straw, is known to calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety, which can also contribute to motion sickness. By combining these herbs, you can get relief from the physical and emotional symptoms of motion sickness. The ginger helps to reduce nausea, the lavender helps to calm the mind and body, and the oat straw helps to regulate the nervous system.
supplementary_avoid: If you have motion sickness, there are some herbal creams you should avoid. One of these herbs is 'Glycyrrhiza glabra' (licorice root). It can cause blood vessels to constrict, which may worsen motion sickness symptoms. Another herb to avoid is 'Artemisia absinthium' (wormwood). It can cause dizziness and nausea, making motion sickness worse. 'Scutellaria baicalensis' (baikal skullcap) is also not recommended as it can increase heart rate and blood pressure, which may exacerbate motion sickness. 'Eucommia ulmoides' (eucommia) can cause anxiety and dizziness, which may worsen motion sickness symptoms. 'Panax quinquefolius' (American ginseng) may cause dizziness and nausea, making motion sickness worse. 'Corydalis rhizoma' (corydalis) can cause dizziness and nausea, which may worsen motion sickness symptoms. 'Paeonia lactiflora' (peony) can increase blood pressure and heart rate, which may exacerbate motion sickness.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger is a natural remedy that can help with motion sickness. It has special properties that help calm the stomach and reduce nausea. You can find ginger in many forms, such as tea, capsules, or even ginger ale. The reason ginger works is because it helps slow down the movement of food through the digestive system, giving your body a chance to catch up with the motion. This can be especially helpful for people who get motion sickness while traveling by car, boat, or plane. You can also try peppermint oil, which has a calming effect on the digestive system. When inhaled, peppermint oil can help reduce nausea and vomiting. Another natural remedy is acupressure bands, which apply pressure to a specific point on the wrist that helps alleviate nausea. These bands work by stimulating a nerve that helps calm the stomach. Some people also find relief with vitamin B6 supplements, which help regulate the body's response to motion.

One common problem is digestive issues, such as nausea and vomiting. Ginger is a well-known herb that can help alleviate these symptoms. It works by reducing inflammation in the stomach and improving digestion.  Another issue that people with motion sickness may face is anxiety and stress. Peppermint is an herb that can help calm the mind and body. It has a soothing effect on the nervous system, which can help reduce anxiety and stress symptoms.  Some people with motion sickness may also experience headaches and fatigue. Feverfew is a herb that can help prevent headaches and reduce fatigue. Additionally, people with motion sickness may have issues with their adrenal glands, which can lead to fatigue and stress.
